Spotify is the main money source for music now.  
But streams don’t pay equally worldwide.  
1M streams in Nigeria = $300.  
1M streams in Sweden = $10,000.  
That’s 33x difference for the same song.

Ever have a problem with @Spotify just randomly stopping playback, or not automatically going to the next track?  I have...

I had tried everything, systematically to hunt down the issue, and I think I found it.

After having turned off everything I could think of, canvas, gapless playback, lower resolution playback... nothing seemed to work.  I would stare at the screen just watching playback stop at approximately 2 seconds before the track would end, and then start, and then stop at the end of the track...

This happened with just about everything from Filter to Billy Eilish, but it didn't happen with a lot of things which kind of confused me.  Some playlists, mostly instrumental ones played back just fine, unless there were parts in which there was a vocalist...

Then I noticed the lyrics.  Lyrics are presented in one of two ways:  Sync'd with the music, and just simply being there... And so I wondered... "Where does Spotify  get it's lyrics from?  Surely there it has it's own database...".  I was mistaken.  Spotify does not actually have a database of lyrics, and uses third party sites and databases, and as far as I can tell, not just one.

It would appear that the main culprit was the Lyric / Audio Syncing Feature.  I'm guessing that Spotify makes a type of request back and forth with almost every track, looking for Lyrics and it has to wait for a response from whomever, which is causing the seemingly random playback stopping.

So, the solution to the problem?  Turn off lyrics and lyrical display, and you should get seemless playback, and no stoppage.
